message:
Add Rust nightly toolchain and dependency configuration

Establish foundational build configuration for the coding-agent-search
project with emphasis on tracking latest Rust nightly and crate releases.

Configuration details:
- Pin to latest Rust nightly via rust-toolchain.toml for access to
  cutting-edge language features and edition 2024 improvements
- Use wildcard (*) version constraints in Cargo.toml to continuously
  track latest stable crate releases, enabling rapid access to bug
  fixes and performance improvements across the dependency tree
- Configure aggressive release optimizations (LTO, single codegen unit,
  size optimization level 'z', symbol stripping) to minimize binary
  footprint for distribution
- Include comprehensive dependency set: ratatui for TUI, tantivy for
  full-text search, rusqlite with bundled SQLite, tokio async runtime,
  crossbeam channels, rayon for parallelism, and supporting crates
  for serialization, logging, and terminal interaction

Dependency highlights:
- ratatui + crossterm: cross-platform TUI with rich widget support
- tantivy: Lucene-like search engine for sub-50ms query latency
- rusqlite (bundled + modern_sqlite): portable SQLite with FTS5
- tokio: async runtime for concurrent connector scanning and indexing
- notify: filesystem watchers for incremental log ingestion
- clap (derive): declarative CLI argument parsing with subcommands
- tracing ecosystem: structured logging with env-based filtering

Simplify .gitignore to minimal essentials, removing verbose sections
inherited from template. Focus on core exclusions: target/, backup
files (*.rs.bk), OS artifacts (.DS_Store), environment config (.env),
build outputs (/dist/, /node_modules/), editor state (.idea/, .vscode/,
*.swp/*.swo), and project coordination metadata (.beads/).

The streamlined .gitignore eliminates 246 lines of overly-specific
patterns (coverage tools, multiple editor variants, OS-specific cruft)
in favor of patterns directly relevant to a Rust CLI application with
minimal external tooling dependencies.

Commit Cargo.lock to ensure reproducible builds across environments,
critical for a binary application where deterministic dependency
resolution prevents "works on my machine" scenarios during development
and issue reproduction.

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 1.1 (toolchain
portability), § 14.1 (scaffolding dependencies), AGENTS.md line 11
(nightly + wildcard policy), README.md (toolchain section).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,48 @@
+[package]
+name = "coding-agent-search"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2024"
+authors = ["Coding Agent Team"]
+description = "Unified TUI search over local coding agent histories"
+license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
+resolver = "2"
+
+[dependencies]
+anyhow = "*"
+thiserror = "*"
+tracing = "*"
+tracing-subscriber = { version = "*", features = ["env-filter", "fmt", "ansi"] }
+tokio = { version = "*", features = ["rt-multi-thread", "macros", "fs", "process", "io-util", "time", "signal"] }
+clap = { version = "*", features = ["derive", "cargo", "env", "unicode", "wrap_help"] }
+indicatif = "*"
+console = "*"
+colored = "*"
+serde = { version = "*", features = ["derive"] }
+serde_json = "*"
+toml = "*"
+directories = "*"
+which = "*"
+shell-words = "*"
+dotenvy = "*"
+notify = "*"
+rusqlite = { version = "*", features = ["bundled", "modern_sqlite"] }
+tantivy = "*"
+rayon = "*"
+crossbeam-channel = "*"
+parking_lot = "*"
+reqwest = { version = "*", default-features = false, features = ["json", "rustls-tls"] }
+ratatui = { version = "*", features = ["crossterm"] }
+crossterm = "*"
+
+[dev-dependencies]
+assert_cmd = "*"
+predicates = "*"
+tempfile = "*"
+insta = { version = "*", features = ["yaml", "json"] }
+
+[profile.release]
+lto = true
+codegen-units = 1
+strip = true
+panic = "abort"
+opt-level = "z"
